reasoning: As of November 2025, the Federal Reserve has only cut the federal funds rate twice in 2025: a 25 bps cut on September 17 (the first of the year) and another 25 bps cut on October 29. This brings the current target range to 3.75% - 4.00%, down 50 bps from the 4.25% - 4.50% rate set in December 2024. For there to be '8+ Fed rate cuts' (where every 25 bps counts as 1 cut) by the end of 2025, the Fed would need to cut rates by an additional 150 bps (6 more cuts of 25 bps) at their final meeting in December 2025. Given that markets are currently pricing in low odds of even a single 25 bps cut in December (with October's meeting 'throwing cold water' on the prospect), a massive 150 bps cut is virtually impossible outside of a catastrophic and unforeseen financial collapse occurring in the span of one month. Because prediction bounds rest at 0.02 - 0.98, the most accurate probability to submit is the minimum allowed value, 2%.
